steps.title: Identify the y-intercept location
steps.content: The y-intercept is where a graph crosses the y-axis. Every point on the y-axis has $x = 0$, so we need to find what $y$-value the curve reaches at $x = 0$.

steps.title: Read the y-value from the graph at x = 0
steps.content: Looking at the graph, locate the y-axis (the vertical line where $x = 0$). Follow the curve to see where it intersects the y-axis. The intersection occurs at $y = -5$. The y-intercept point is therefore $(0, -5)$, which matches choice B — B is correct.
